nodes.label: Hermes AI Headless CMS
nodes.rationale: Multi-tenant, AI-powered headless CMS utilizing Payload CMS monolith and Python FastAPI AI microservice.

nodes.label: Hermes AI Constitution
nodes.rationale: Non-negotiable architectural principles (Multi-tenancy, AI-First, API-First, TDD, DX, DDD, Hybrid) to guide all development.

nodes.label: Alexandria Design System
nodes.rationale: High-end editorial visual layout system prioritizing serif authority, generous whitespace, surface-layer hierarchy, and no-line boundaries.

nodes.label: Content Management Engine Monolith
nodes.rationale: Next.js & Payload CMS monolith for logical tenant isolation and custom admin views.

nodes.label: Content Authoring Service Microservice
nodes.rationale: FastAPI Python service for LangChain multi-provider orchestrations.

nodes.label: Langfuse UI stack
nodes.rationale: Enterprise-grade LLM observability, distributed tracing, and prompt version control.

JS/TS SDK v4 to v5 Upgrade
Storage Adapters (S3, Azure, GCS, R2, Vercel Blob)

Speckit Git Create Feature Branch Command
Principle I: Multi-tenancy by Default
nodes.rationale: Every feature, data model, and API endpoint MUST be designed with multi-tenancy in mind from day one. Data isolation for the CMS MUST be enforced logically within a shared database using the Payload CMS multi-tenant plugin patterns.

nodes.label: Principle II: AI as a First-Class Citizen
nodes.rationale: The conversational AI agent is not an add-on; it is the primary interface for content schema design and initial content drafting.

nodes.label: Principle III: API-First Content Delivery
nodes.rationale: The CMS is strictly headless. Content delivery MUST be exposed via robust, read-only JSON APIs.

nodes.label: Principle IV: Test-First TDD
nodes.rationale: Test-Driven Development (TDD) is mandatory. Tests must be written before implementation, focusing heavily on tenant isolation (logical boundaries), AI orchestration, and API response performance.

nodes.label: Principle V: Developer Experience DX
nodes.rationale: The platform must provide a frictionless experience for developers building on top of it. This includes managed front-end starter templates, comprehensive documentation, and predictable API structures.

nodes.label: Principle VI: Strict Domain-Driven Design
nodes.rationale: The codebase MUST adhere strictly to Domain-Driven Design principles, especially for backend microservices outside the core CMS. Business logic must be encapsulated within distinct Bounded Contexts.

nodes.label: Principle VII: Hybrid Architecture
nodes.rationale: The core Content Management System MUST be built as a modular monolith using Payload CMS. However, heavy backend processing, specifically the AI Agents and orchestration logic, MUST be implemented as independent microservices adhering to strict DDD principles.

nodes.label: Content Management Engine (CMS Monolith)
nodes.rationale: Payload CMS monolith is utilized to handle UI, collections, multi-tenancy, and session persistence in a logical multi-tenancy model to guarantee 0% cross-tenant data bleed.

nodes.label: SSE Content Drafting Flow
nodes.rationale: The flow utilizes unidirectional HTTP Server-Sent Events (SSE) where Next.js proxies user actions to FastAPI and streams structured events like TEXT_DELTA, FIELD_START, FIELD_COMPLETE, and DRAFT_COMPLETE.

nodes.label: Single-User Session Locking & Recovery
nodes.rationale: To prevent concurrent editing conflicts while preserving data safety, a compound partial unique index on PostgreSQL ensures only one user has an active session per content schema inside a tenant, with dynamic lockout checks and a 24-hour recovery window.

nodes.label: Distributed Tracing & Prompt Management
nodes.rationale: Next.js establishes a parent trace in Langfuse, propagating it to FastAPI via headers, while decoupling system prompts into a dynamic catalog hosted in Langfuse.

nodes.label: Postgres-Backed Sliding-Window Rate Limiting
nodes.rationale: Limits user actions to 10 per minute using a global user-scoped collection, bypassing tenant filters to control AI credit consumption and platform costs.

nodes.label: Self-Healing Output Parsing
nodes.rationale: Intercepts formatting/validation errors from LLMs and applies a secondary corrective recovery chain to ensure reliable JSON payload generation matching the target Payload schema.

nodes.label: Decision: Hybrid Language Stack
nodes.rationale: Python (3.12+) is selected for the AI Microservice as the industry standard for AI, ML, and data engineering, offering the most robust ecosystem (LangChain, LangGraph) and broad support for LLM providers. TypeScript (Node.js 24+) is used for the CMS monolith to utilize the Next.js/Payload ecosystem.

nodes.label: Decision: Logical Multi-Tenancy
nodes.rationale: Payload's official multi-tenant plugin handles mapping and access control in the CMS, while the AI microservice respects these tenant boundaries by associating all sessions and generated data with the incoming tenantId.

nodes.label: Decision: Inter-Service Communication
nodes.rationale: Use asynchronous events via Message Broker (Kafka/RabbitMQ) for generation events, combined with synchronous internal REST APIs for CMS-AI microservice interactions.

nodes.label: Multi-tenant Headless CMS Specification Quality Checklist
Content Delivery and Management API Contracts
Tenant Management Technical Decisions and Rationale
Define Content Types with AI Copilot Quickstart Guide
Define Content Types Technical Decisions and Rationale
Define Content Types Specification Quality Checklist
Multi-tenant Logical vs Physical Isolation
nodes.rationale: Logical isolation is implemented via Payload CMS ACL filters per tenant ID, while physical isolation (separate databases) is used to completely isolate schemas and heavy AI workloads.

nodes.label: Domain Mapping Strategy
nodes.rationale: Store a domains array field directly within the Tenants collection. This avoids separate collection lookup overhead and simplifies integration with Payload's multi-tenant plugin.

nodes.label: Soft Delete and Archiving Strategy
nodes.rationale: Mark tenant as Archived rather than hard deleting, hiding all content from APIs but preserving data for recovery.

nodes.label: Tenant Hostname Resolution
nodes.rationale: Perform hostname lookups cached in memory or indexed queries in PostgreSQL to achieve tenant resolution in <50ms.

nodes.label: Dynamic Content Validation Strategy
nodes.rationale: Validate user input at the application layer in beforeValidate hooks against the active ContentType schema. Avoid hot-reloading Next.js/Payload configurations in production.

nodes.label: AI Suggestion Self-Correction Loop
nodes.rationale: FastAPI microservice validates LLM output using Pydantic. If validation fails, it triggers a self-correction prompt (up to 3 retries) with specific parser errors before returning to the UI.

nodes.label: Destructive Schema Change Lock
nodes.rationale: Block destructive changes (deletions, incompatible type updates) on published Content Types if they already contain Content Items, to prevent data corruption.

nodes.label: Optimistic Concurrency Control
nodes.rationale: Compare updatedAt timestamp from client-submitted data with DB value on save. Reject with 409 Conflict if mismatch to prevent last-write-wins collisions.

nodes.label: Database-level Drafting Session Lock
nodes.rationale: Implement a partial unique index on (tenant, contentType) where status = 'active' to enforce a single active drafting session per content type, eliminating race conditions.

nodes.label: Markdown-to-Lexical JSON Conversion
nodes.rationale: Use @payloadcms/richtext-lexical's $convertFromMarkdownString with a headless Lexical editor instance on the CMS Engine server side (Next.js API route), not in the browser. Server-side conversion ensures correct node resolution using the full Payload editor config without shipping server-only config/node types to browser.

nodes.label: SSE Streaming Architecture
nodes.rationale: Two-hop SSE relay architecture: Content Authoring Service (FastAPI StreamingResponse) -> CMS Engine (Next.js API route proxying SSE, adding auth & tenant scoping) -> Browser (fetch with ReadableStream for POST bodies).

nodes.label: LangChain Image Generation Tool
nodes.rationale: Define a generate_image LangChain tool in the Content Authoring Service. The tool calls DALL-E/Stability AI, returns a temp URL, and emits SSE. CMS Engine stream-downloads the image to Payload Media collection to prevent OOM errors.

nodes.label: DraftingSession Locking and Lifecycle
nodes.rationale: Implement single-user lock via database-level unique constraint (partial unique index on tenant, contentType where status = 'active') + beforeChange hook. 10-minute inactivity timeout transitions to expired status, retaining data in a 24-hour recovery window before permanent purge.

nodes.label: Rate Limiting Strategy
nodes.rationale: Application-level rate limiting (10 AI requests/minute/user) in CMS Engine proxy using a Postgres-backed sliding window counter in a global (non-tenant-scoped) collection (AIRateLimits) with a compound index on (userId, timestamp).

nodes.label: Auto-Save Strategy
nodes.rationale: Debounced client-side auto-save (500ms after last change) via PATCH /api/drafting-sessions/:id. Saves rich-text body directly as Lexical JSON. Pausing generation triggers immediate auto-save of partial streamed text.

nodes.label: Version Snapshot Strategy
nodes.rationale: Store snapshots as a JSON array field (versions) on the DraftingSession document, capped at the last 10 versions. Rollback is executed server-side via dedicated POST /api/ai-drafting/sessions/[id]/rollback.

nodes.label: Atomic Promotion Transaction Safety
nodes.rationale: Wrap ContentItem creation and DraftingSession deletion in a single database transaction (payload.db.beginTransaction) to ensure atomicity. If either fails, neither persists.

nodes.label: StyleModifier Collection Design
nodes.rationale: Tenant-scoped collection style-modifiers containing name, description, systemPrompt, and isDefault tone checkbox, injected verbatim into LLM context.

nodes.label: AIAuditLog Collection Design
nodes.rationale: Dedicated tenant-scoped ai-audit-logs collection to track prompt/completion tokens, duration, model, provider, and estimated cost in USD microdollars.

nodes.label: Content Drafting Bounded Context
nodes.rationale: Create a new content_drafting bounded context under src/domain/content_drafting/ with value objects ContentDraft and DraftField, complying with strict Domain-Driven Design principles.

nodes.label: Locale Awareness
nodes.rationale: Locale is a first-class parameter passed throughout the request chain. AI drafts and refines strictly in the current active locale selected in the editor UI. Markdown-to-Lexical conversion uses activeLocale.

nodes.label: API Route Placement
nodes.rationale: Custom AI proxy and session routes are placed outside the app/(payload)/ route group, under app/api/ to avoid routing conflicts with Payload's catch-all route.

nodes.label: Micro-Frontend Event Bus
nodes.rationale: Type-safe PubSub event bus utility using window.dispatchEvent/addEventListener to enable real-time multi-component sync.

nodes.label: MFE Error Boundary
nodes.rationale: React error boundary to wrap 3rd-party micro-frontend injection components with strict 3000ms timeout script load and fallback UI.

nodes.label: FastAPI AI Tool Circuit Breaker
nodes.rationale: Fault tolerance on external 3rd-party LangChain tool execution using pybreaker to transition to OPEN on 3 failures or 5000ms timeouts.

nodes.label: Block Registry API
nodes.rationale: Synchronous POST /api/blocks/register endpoint in the CMS monolith using Tenant API keys for immediate validation against the BuildingBlock collection.

nodes.label: Deployment Webhook Trigger
nodes.rationale: Webhook-based signaling via modified HostedSites configurations. Logs a TemplateDeployment when pushing template configuration to live frontend.

nodes.label: Hydrated Block Tree Resolution
nodes.rationale: CMS-side resolution fetches Content Item, resolves associated PageTemplate, maps data to BlockInstances, and returns hydrated block tree.

nodes.label: Optimistic Concurrency Control for PageTemplates
nodes.rationale: OCC via version field and If-Unmodified-Since header validation in hooks to prevent concurrent editing overwrites.

nodes.label: Dnd-Kit Drag and Drop Refinements
nodes.rationale: PointerSensor with 8px activation distance and library- prefix calculations using instanceId for Sortable canvas items.

nodes.label: A2A, MCP & A2UI Integration Implementation Plan
A2A, MCP & A2UI Integration Research & Rationale
A2A, MCP & A2UI Integration Developer & Operator Manual
Internal API Key Validation
nodes.rationale: API key validation is offloaded synchronously to Next.js CMS engine via X-Internal-Secret to maintain central authority and avoid direct DB reads from the AI microservice.

nodes.label: SSE (Server-Sent Events) Transport
nodes.rationale: Implemented for cloud-based integrations like ChatGPT where stdio is not possible, adhering to standard Model Context Protocol web/HTTP transport.

nodes.label: Stdio Transport
nodes.rationale: Used by local developer desktop tools like Claude Desktop, running the server as a subprocess.

nodes.label: Alexandria A2UI Component Schema
nodes.rationale: Lightweight JSON component schema mapping semantic Alexandria design system tokens, keeping the Python microservice decoupled from visual details.

nodes.label: Template Builder Agent Research & Technical Decisions
TemplateBuilderState
nodes.rationale: Custom LangGraph state schema tracking input HTML, generated outputs, validation errors, and retry logs.

nodes.label: TemplateBuilderOutput
nodes.rationale: Pydantic structured output model to ensure LLM yields a clean, parseable Content Type schema and parameterized HTML template.

nodes.label: Cache-Busting Strategy
nodes.rationale: Unique hashes appended to generated media asset filenames to force Next.js router and browser cache invalidation instantly.

nodes.label: Relational Null Queries
nodes.rationale: Use { field: { equals: null } } instead of { exists: false } for empty relation checks in PostgreSQL-backed Payload collections to prevent query execution errors.

nodes.label: TemplateHistoryPage Custom View
nodes.rationale: Implemented as a standalone custom view at /admin/templates/history reusing Alexandria design system components to ensure visual consistency.

nodes.label: Template Deployment History Tasks
nodes.rationale: MVP First approach: Group tasks by user story to enable incremental delivery and independent testing. First deliver the basic list view (User Story 1) which provides the core value, then add search, filtering, and pagination as incremental enhancements with TDD cycles.

Tenant-Scoped API Access Integration Test
nodes.rationale: Ensures logical tenant isolation is enforced at the API level before core view implementation

nodes.label: Specification Readiness Check
nodes.rationale: Validate specification completeness and quality before planning, ensuring zero leaks of implementation details
